comparisons.visual: Late 18th century. Snow packed hard underfoot. A cold northern breeze cuts the air. ROBERT WALTON, twenty-eight, lean and bright-eyed, walks in heavy furs. The …
Walton writes on, fervor rising.
Back to the letter. Walton's face is open, almost pleading with the page.
He stops. The room is empty. The silence presses.
He seals the letter.
Victor wrapped in blankets, fevered, slowly mending under Walton's care. Walton sits by him with broth and quiet talk.
A modest carriage pulls away from Geneva's walls. Beaufort does not look back. Caroline steadies him.
Years later. Young Victor, about five-slim, dark hair, intent curious face-walks with Caroline past poor cottages. Alphonse has gone ahead toward Milan. A disco…
Morning. Caroline presents Elizabeth. Young Victor receives her with childish seriousness-as a possession of his own to protect, love, and cherish.
Victor's disappointment hardens into secret resolve.
Alphonse, broken but upright. Victor prepares for university. Elizabeth and Clerval bid him farewell with tears and forced cheer.
Candles burnt low. Victor still at work as the high steeple outside pales toward morning. Stars disappear in the light. His application no longer fluctuating-ar…
He closes the book. Resolve hardens.
Walton leans forward, eagerness naked in his eyes. Victor meets that look and stops him with a raised hand.
A solitary chamber, almost a cell, separated by gallery and stair. Filthy creation: instruments, vats, the vast unfinished form under cloth. Victor's eyeballs s…
Victor wanders, unable to return. Morning light finds him exhausted near an inn. A coach rattles in. HENRY CLERVAL springs down, radiant.
Victor nods almost imperceptibly-memory of a frank, happy face.
Books, specimens, soft light. M. WALDMAN-kind, silvered, warm-clasps Victor's hand, then Henry's.
Krempe launches into a eulogy on himself. Victor breathes again.
Victor turns, radiant, and throws an arm toward the open sky.
Thunder. In a flash of lightning Victor sees among the trees a gigantic figure-the Creature-climbing the rocks of a nearby precipice with superhuman speed.
A murmur of horror fills the court. Justine's countenance alters-surprise, horror, misery.
A murmur of approbation-excited by Elizabeth's generous interference, not in favour of Justine. Public indignation turns on the accused with renewed violence. V…
Justine on a straw pallet, eyes swollen. Elizabeth and Victor enter. Justine rises and falls into Elizabeth's arms.
Weeks of gloom. Victor wanders alone, unable to rest. The family removes to Belrive. He takes the boat at night and drifts under the stars, sometimes tempted by…
He pauses on a winding, rain streaking his face.
Victor's rage falters into a bitter necessity of hearing.
A mean shed built against a cottage of neat plainness. The Creature crawls in, finds it empty of occupant, and lies on straw, grateful for shelter.
Morning. Agatha opens the door-astonished at the great pile of wood. Felix joins her; surprise breaks his habitual sadness. He does not go to the forest that da…
Thaw. The Creature comes upon a transparent pool. He leans to drink-and starts back from his own reflection. Yellow eyes. Scarred yellow skin. Enormous frame. H…
Autumn light. DE LACEY sits alone with his guitar. He plays a mournful air, then lays it aside. A knock.
The Creature howls into the dark.
Justine asleep in the barn, exhausted from searching. The Creature sees her-sees a way to wound his creator further. He plants the miniature in the folds of her…
The Creature departs over the ice with the speed of lightning. Victor is left alone in the hut as the sun sets behind the snowy precipices.
Victor pauses over the unfinished female. Imagination shows him a race of devils; the female refusing the compact; two monsters loose upon the world. He looks u…
Father and son on deck. Victor is a shadow. Alphonse tries to speak of Elizabeth, of hope. Victor hears only the Creature's promise.
Elizabeth retires to the chamber. Victor paces the outer rooms and passages, pistol in hand, determined to meet his enemy alone. A dreadful scream from the bedr…
Victor springs up, but the Creature is already fled.
The ice closes harder about the ship. The crew, led by Willoughby's anxious courage and Fletcher's quiet concern, beg Walton to turn south when the floes open.
His eyes close. A last breath. Victor Frankenstein dies. Walton weeps over the body of his friend.
